newsSince: One fresh operational milestone in window: Nofar's fully-owned 169 MW Iepuresti solar park in Romania (largest of its kind there, expandable to 400 MW) reached commercial operation in June 2026, estimated ~EUR 19M first-year revenue / ~EUR 16M EBITDA, with utility-scale storage integration underway; plus a 22 MW Israel solar/storage EPC agreement with Airengy. The -15.8% price move since June 9 is profit-taking/flow, separate from these positive fundamentals.

sources.label: Romania's largest PV park online, by Nofar (Romania Insider)
Airengy to build Nofar Energy Israel solar+storage

verdicts.rankRationale: On the test-time-scaling/power-buildout lens, bringing the 169 MW Romania park (->400 MW) into commercial operation with ~EUR 16M EBITDA plus new Israel solar+storage capacity is a real generation/electricity-bucket add. +2 fundamental credit (price drop ignored per mandate); not enough to leapfrog peers, so hold at 18.
verdicts.bull: New operational MW + storage build-out grows renewable generation feeding AI power demand.
verdicts.neutral: Small contribution vs total scaling-power basket; project, not structural shift.
verdicts.bear: Israeli-listed, leveraged developer; execution/financing and FX risk.
